migration_policy: additive until 1.x; breaking changes require migration note, fixture update, backwards-compatibility decision, explicit reviewer approval
title: CC variant candidate (untrusted API output)
description: The UNTRUSTED intake contract for PR-10.4b: the raw JSON a generator returns from the locked variant prompt, BEFORE any validation. Exactly five keys — the candidate can never assert governance fields (status, source, fact_ids, objective, correct_index...); the intake derives those from governed stores. The correct choice is never named: it is DERIVED as the single choice index absent from distractor_misconceptions, then corroborated against the semantic lock and the full PR-10.4a gate set. self_check is verified strictly but never trusted alone (reviewer rule). Enforcement lives in cc_spine.variant_intake.parse_candidate; this schema is the contract document.

stem_variant.description: The reworded scenario/stem. Must not near-duplicate the source stem (gate 12) or any holdout stem (intake gate I8).

choices_variant.description: Reworded choices in authoring order (rendered via the PR-10.3 presentation shuffle). Must be pairwise distinct; count must equal the source item's (intake gate I7).

correct_choice_concept.description: The correct answer as a CONCEPT (never a display letter/index). Must echo the request's correct_concept (intake gate I6) — a divergence signals the generator mislabeled which choice is correct.

distractor_misconceptions.description: Maps each WRONG choice index (canonical decimal string) to the misconception id it embodies. Exactly ONE in-range index must be absent — that index is the correct choice (intake gate I4). Ids must be within the request's targets (gate I5); this map is ANSWER-BEARING and is stored in the isolated draft key file, never the draft itself.

self_check.description: The generator's own attestation. Any value other than literal true rejects the candidate — and a passing self_check is still fully re-validated locally (never trusted alone).
